title: Dialog Schema
description: Schema for Dialog domain modeling: attributes, concepts, rules, formulas, and relations.

Attribute.description: A relation elevated with domain-specific invariants. An attribute's identity is structural: (domain, name, type, cardinality).

domain.description: Domain of the attribute (e.g. 'diy.cook'). In YAML notation, inferred from the enclosing key.

name.description: Name of the attribute (e.g. 'quantity'). In YAML notation, inferred from the label.

cardinality.description: Cardinality of the attribute. Defaults to 'one' when omitted.

assert.description: Asserted type of the attribute value. If omitted, any type is allowed.

Cardinality.description: 'one' means asserting a new value retracts the prior claim. 'many' means new claims accumulate alongside existing ones.

Assert.description: The asserted value type for an attribute. Currently only scalar types are supported. Concept references and symbol enumerations are planned future extensions.

ScalarType.description: Built-in scalar types from the dialog domain. 'Text' is shorthand for 'dialog/Text', etc.

ConceptRef.description: Reference to a concept. Can be dot-prefixed (.Ingredient) for current domain, or fully qualified (diy.cook/Ingredient).

SymbolEnum.description: [Future extension , not yet supported] A fixed set of symbol values (e.g. [:tsp, :mls]). Each symbol is domain-qualified at resolution time.

Concept.description: A composition of attributes sharing an entity. An entity matches a concept if and only if it has claims satisfying all required attributes.

where.description: Required fields. An entity must have claims satisfying all these attributes to match.

option.description: [Future extension , not yet supported] Optional fields. The entity may or may not have claims for these attributes.

NamedRelations.description: Map of field names to attribute descriptors (inline) or attribute references (string paths).

AttributeRef.description: Reference to a pre-defined attribute. '.' means same-named attribute under the current domain. '.name' resolves within current domain. 'domain/name' is fully qualified.

Rule.description: A deductive rule that derives conclusions from existing information through pattern matching, computation, and logical composition.

assert.description: The conclusion: a concept instance the rule derives when its body is satisfied.

when.description: Conjunction of premises. All must be satisfied by the same variable bindings.

unless.description: Exclusion patterns. If any can be satisfied, the result is filtered out (negation as failure).

Premise.description: A single premise in a rule body. Combines an assertion (what to match) with named term bindings (how to bind variables).

assert.description: What to match: a concept (inline definition), an attribute (inline definition), a formula reference (e.g. 'formula/Sum'), or a constraint reference (e.g. 'Equality').

with.description: Named terms mapping field names to variables or constants. For attributes, 'this' binds the entity and the attribute name binds the value. For concepts, names correspond to the concept's attribute names. For formulas and constraints, names correspond to their parameter names.

formula/Sum.description: Adds two integers: is = of + with.

is.description: Derived: sum of the two operands.

formula/Difference.description: Subtracts second from first (saturating at 0): is = of - subtract.

formula/Product.description: Multiplies two integers: is = of * times.

formula/Quotient.description: Divides first by second: is = of / by. Produces no result when divisor is zero.

formula/Modulo.description: Remainder of division: is = of % by. Produces no result when divisor is zero.

formula/Concatenate.description: Joins two strings: is = first ++ second.

formula/Like.description: Glob pattern match. Produces a result only when the pattern matches. '*' matches any sequence, '?' matches a single character.

is.description: Derived: the matched text (empty if no match).

formula/ToString.description: Converts any value to its string representation.

formula/ParseNumber.description: Parses a string as an integer. Produces no result if parsing fails.

Constraint.description: Constraints restrict variable bindings within a rule body.

EqualityConstraint.description: Asserts two terms must hold equal values. Can filter (both bound), infer (one bound, one free), or fail (neither bound).

Term.description: A term is either a variable or a constant value.

Variable.description: A query variable. Variables are bound by the query engine. The same variable appearing in multiple positions requires unification. A blank variable { "?": {} } matches any value without binding.

name.description: Variable name. When omitted, acts as a blank (wildcard) that matches any value without binding.

Constant.description: A concrete value: string, number, or boolean.

Claim.description: A claim in the associative layer. The fundamental unit of information, composed of a relation (the), an entity (of), a value (is), and provenance (cause).

of.description: The entity this claim is about.

is.description: The value being linked to the entity through this relation.

cause.description: Provenance describing who produced this claim and when.

Provenance.description: Provenance of a claim, capturing when and where it was produced. Establishes partial order across a distributed system.

by.description: DID of the operator or session authority that produced the claim.

period.description: Coordinated time component: last synchronization cycle.

moment.description: Uncoordinated local time component: moment within a period.
